texto: A dominant ideology represents the view of a dominant group, often by making the existing order seem inevitable. Thus, by depicting motherhood as natural, a patriarchal ideology of mothering locks women into biological reproduction, and denies them identities and selfhood outside mothering.
explicacao: Citação de Evelyn Nakano Glenn sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “A dominant ideology represents the view of a dominant group, often by making the existing order s…” articula reflexao, ideology, motherhood em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como motherhood e mothering.

frase_original: A dominant ideology represents the view of a dominant group, often by making the existing order seem inevitable. Thus, by depicting motherhood as natural, a patriarchal ideology of mothering locks women into biological reproduction, and denies them identities and selfhood outside mothering.
